day on the job when I got out there and that first door I knocked on right that the first woman who answered I ended up selling a 13 boxes of meat there's like she's probably still eating it 30 years later right 13 boxes of meat all right which is massive called the baker's dozen right and I still remember to this day knowing like that when that she opened that that big smile that eye contact the way I engaged her I knew how important was it just so something that's how my brain was wired right and I can tell you that a lot of my success in door-to-door sales was that making that sort of that amazing first impression with my eye contact my smile how close I stood from the door or in someone's office you know what was my posture was I leaning foes I leaning back what was I even wearing all those sort of things when you're in person they make just this incredible difference in your ability to get into rapport in your ability to establish trust and ultimately in your ability to get your point across I persuade someone to say yes
